issues: Code imports fetch_reddit_activity from crazy.adapters.reddit_activity as required.
Code uses adapter output columns 'date', 'mention_count', 'price', 'sma_20', 'sma_14_mentions' which are NOT part of the adapter contract (adapter returns only 'date', 'posts', 'comments').
Signal logic assumes columns 'mention_count', 'price', 'sma_20', 'sma_14_mentions' exist in data, but adapter contract does not provide these metrics.

Signal logic correctly implements entry and exit conditions per spec intent (mention count spike >1.5 * 14-day avg and price > 20-day SMA for entry; mention count < 14-day avg or price < 20-day SMA for exit).
Position sizing correctly allocates 5% per position on BUY (RISK_ON) and no allocation on SELL (RISK_OFF) or HOLD.
Overall fail due to mismatch between adapter contract outputs and code's expected data columns.
